Ayah

سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۱)
Whatever there is in the heavens and whatever there is in the earth glorifies Allah, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (1)

Key Points

In the Quran, the word Tasbih (تسبیح - glorification) has been used approximately 85 times. In Salat (prayer) too, the subject of glorifying Allah is prominently seen during the various states of Ruku' (bowing), Sujud (prostration), and Qiyam (standing). Linguistically, Tasbih means swimming and rapid movement in water and air, and technically (in terminology), it means declaring Allah free from defects and hastening in His worship. Allah possesses two types of attributes: Thubutiyyah (affirmative) and Salbiyyah (negative/privative). Thubutiyyah describes Allah's perfections, such as Knowledge and Power, while Salbiyyah declares Allah free from any form of inability, ignorance, or need. Tasbih relates to the Salbiyyah attributes, and Hamd (praise) relates to the Thubutiyyah attributes. In certain instances in the Quran and Salat, the glorification and praise of Allah have come together. "یسبّح بحمده" (1), "سبحان ربّى الاعلى و بحمده" (Glory be to my Lord, the Most High, and praise be to Him), and this is the combination of the Thubutiyyah and Salbiyyah attributes, meaning that the speaker both declares Allah far removed from every defect and praises Him for receiving divine favors.

The Glorification of Creatures Some have said that the intent behind the glorification of creatures is Takwini (existential/natural) glorification, not Tashri'i (legislative/verbal). That is, all creatures glorify and declare Allah free from imperfection through the language of their state, not the language of speech, because the creation of every being is proof of Allah's Knowledge, Wisdom, and Power, and His being free from any form of ignorance, futility, or inability. Therefore, there is no need for creatures to glorify Him verbally; rather, their very existence is the glorification of Allah.

However, it seems that the Quran's intent regarding the glorification of creatures is glorification by speech, because: 1. If the wise creation of beings were the sign of glorification, we would understand this sign, whereas in another Ayah, He says: You do not understand their glorification. "لاتفقهون تسبیحهم" (2) 2. If creation were the sign of glorification, like a beautiful building praising its engineer and architect, then why does He say: "كلّ قد علم صلاته و تسبیحه" (3) (Each one knows its prayer and its glorification)? Creatures are aware of their prayer and glorification, and this is based on perception and consciousness, not existential (takwini) and unconscious action.

The glorification of creatures toward Allah is continuous. In some Surahs, it appears in the past tense: "سبّح" (glorified), and in some, in the present/future tense: "یسبّح" (glorifies).

1) Isra, 44. 2) Isra, 44.

Messages

1- The creatures of the heavens and the earth possess a type of consciousness and perception. "سبّح للّه" 2- Even if some humans are not devoted to glorification, the entire universe glorifies Allah. "سبّح للّه..." 3- It is Allah's Might (Izzah) and Wisdom (Hikmah) that draws everyone to glorification. "سبّح للّه... و هو العزیز الحكیم" 4- Although power causes ordinary individuals to lose balance, Allah possesses power, and all His actions are also wise. "العزیز الحكیم"

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Hashr This Surah has twenty-four verses and was revealed in Medina. It has been named Al-Hashr (The Gathering) due to the word "حَشر" which appears in the second verse. Of course, the intent is not the gathering on the Day of Judgment, but rather the assembly of people for migration (or expulsion). The beginning and end of this Surah are about the glorification of all beings in existence for Allah, and its content is mostly about the cooperation of the hypocrites with the Jews of Medina in plotting against the Muslims, regarding which the Quran states: their schemes will lead nowhere and will bring them nothing but humiliation and defeat.

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَخْرَجَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِن دِيَـٰرِهِمْ لِأَوَّلِ ٱلْحَشْرِ ۚ مَا ظَنَنتُمْ أَن يَخْرُجُوا۟ ۖ وَظَنُّوٓا۟ أَنَّهُم مَّانِعَتُهُمْ حُصُونُهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ فَأَتَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَمْ يَحْتَسِبُوا۟ ۖ وَقَذَفَ فِى قُلُوبِهِمُ ٱلرُّعْبَ ۚ يُخْرِبُونَ بُيُوتَهُم بِأَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَيْدِى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ فَٱعْتَبِرُوا۟ يَـٰٓأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرِ (۲)
It is He who expelled the faithless belonging to the People of the Book from their homes at the outset of [their] en masse banishment. You did not think that they would go out, and they thought their fortresses would protect them from Allah. But Allah came at them from whence they did not suppose and He cast terror into their hearts. They demolish their houses with their own hands and the hands of the faithful. So take lesson, O you who have insight! (2)

Key Points

The word Hashr means gathering and driving out from the place of residence. Fa’tabirū (take a lesson) comes from the root ‘abr, meaning passing beyond the apparent and reaching the inner reality. The word ‘ibārat (expression/phrase) means passing beyond the word (the literal) and reaching the meaning, and ta‘bīr-e khwāb (dream interpretation) is passing from the apparent reality of the dream to its inner reality.

Three tribes of Jews, named Banu Nadir, Banu Qurayzah, and Banu Qaynuqa‘, migrated to Medina and settled there, and they made a covenant of non-aggression with the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. However, after the Battle of Uhud, a group of them joined the polytheists of Mecca and conspired against the Muslims. The Prophet became aware and confronted them. One of their leaders, Ka‘b ibn al-Ashraf, was killed, causing instability among the Jews, and they took refuge in a fortress. The Muslims, by the Prophet’s command, besieged the fortress. After several days of siege and fighting, the Jews agreed to leave Medina. As they left, they took with them whatever possessions they could, and what they could not take, such as their houses, they destroyed so that they would not fall intact into the hands of the Muslims. (4)

In the Quran, fear cast into the hearts of the enemy is repeatedly counted as one of the Divine aids, including: “qadhafa fī qulūbihim al-ru‘b” (He cast terror into their hearts), “sa’ulqī fī qulūb alladhīna kafarū al-ru‘b” (I shall cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve) (5), “sanuqī fī qulūb alladhīna kafarū al-ru‘b” (We shall cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve) (6). Yes, fear causes the enemy to flee and leads to the victory of the Muslims without bloodshed. We read in a Hadith: Three armies will come to aid Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام: an army of angels, an army of believers, and the creation of terror and dread in the hearts of the enemies. (7)

Contemplation and taking a lesson is an act of worship, just as we read in narrations: “kāna aktharu ‘ibādati Abī Dharr al-tafakkar wa al-i‘tibār” (The greatest act of worship of Abu Dharr was contemplation and taking a lesson). (8)

Neither is Divine mercy confined to our calculations, “yarzuquhu min ḥaythu lā yaḥtasib” (He provides for him from where He does not expect) (9), nor is His wrath. “fa’atāhum Allāhu min ḥaythu lam yaḥtasibū” (Then Allah came upon them from where they did not expect). Allah controls the hearts. Sometimes He sends down tranquility: “anzala al-sakīnata fī qulūb al-mu’minīn” (He sent down tranquility into the hearts of the believers) (10), and sometimes He casts fear: “qadhafa fī qulūbihim al-ru‘b” (He cast terror into their hearts).

3) Noor, 41. 4) Tafsir Namūneh. 5) Anfal, 12. 6) Aal-e Imran, 151. 7) Tafsir Namūneh from Ithbāt al-Hudā, Vol. 7, p. 124. 8) Khiṣāl al-Ṣadūq. 9) Talaq, 3. 10) Fath, 4.

Messages

1- The wrath of Allah towards the covenant-breaking disbelieving Jews is wise. “Al-‘Azīz al-Ḥakīm huwa alladhī akhraja” (The All-Mighty, the All-Wise, it is He Who expelled).

2- A sign of Allah’s Might (‘Izzah) is the scattering and destruction of the treacherous and conspiring Jews. “Al-‘Azīz al-Ḥakīm huwa alladhī akhraja” (The All-Mighty, the All-Wise, it is He Who expelled).

3- We must attribute all victories to Him. “Huwa alladhī akhraja alladhīna kafarū...” (It is He Who expelled those who disbelieved...).

4- The account of the conspirators among the People of the Book is separate from the account of the rest of them. “alladhīna kafarū min Ahl al-Kitāb” (those who disbelieved from the People of the Book).

5- Exile from one’s homeland is one of the Divine punishments. “Huwa alladhī akhraja... min diyārihim” (It is He Who expelled... from their homes).

6- Do not consider the power of any person or group to be permanent. “mā ẓanantum an yakhrujū” (you did not think that they would leave).

7- The punishment for one who violates social commitments and covenants is that he should be expelled from his homeland. “an yakhrujū...” (that they would leave...).

8- The calculations and equipment of the disbelievers are ineffective and useless against the Divine Will. “ẓannū annahum māni‘atuhum ḥuṣūnuhum” (they thought that their fortresses would protect them).

9- In war and Jihad against the disbelievers, all calculations should not be based solely on material equipment and resources. “fa’atāhum Allāhu min ḥaythu lam yaḥtasibū” (Then Allah came upon them from where they did not expect).

10- The destruction of enemy buildings is permissible in certain cases. “yukhribūna buyūtahum bi’aydīhim wa aydī al-mu’minīn” (they were destroying their houses with their own hands and the hands of the believers).

11- Where the Will of Allah exists, friend and foe participate in the same action. “yukhribūna buyūtahum bi’aydīhim wa aydī al-mu’minīn” (they were destroying their houses with their own hands and the hands of the believers).

12- Allah is both the Creator of means and the Nullifier of means; He makes fear a means (to victory) and renders fortresses ineffective as means (of protection). “qadhafa fī qulūbihim al-ru‘b” (He cast terror into their hearts).

13- Studying history is not entertainment; it is a means of taking a lesson. “Fa‘tabirū...” (So take a lesson...). History is a passage, not a stopping place.

14- Utilizing history requires insight. “Fa‘tabirū yā ulī al-abṣār” (So take a lesson, O people of insight).

Ayah

وَلَوْلَآ أَن كَتَبَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْجَلَآءَ لَعَذَّبَهُمْ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ عَذَابُ ٱلنَّارِ (۳)
If Allah had not ordained banishment for them, He would have surely punished them in this world, and in the Hereafter, there is the punishment of the Fire for them. (3)

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ شَآقُّوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ ۖ وَمَن يُشَآقِّ ٱللَّهَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (٤)
That is because they defied Allah and His Apostle; and whoever defies Allah, Allah is indeed severe in retribution. (4)

Key Points

Jalā' (جلاء) means leaving one's land and homeland due to affliction and calamity. Shāqqū (شاقّوا) is derived from Shaqq (شقّ), meaning to create a rift, separation, confrontation, and enmity. In Sūrat al-Mujādilah, Ayah 21, He said: «كتب اللّه لاغلبّن أنا و رسلى» (Allah has decreed: 'I and My Messengers will surely prevail'), and in this Ayah, He says: «كتب اللّه علیهم الجلاء» (Allah decreed exile upon them). This means both victory and exile are by Divine Will. A verse similar to Ayah 4 appears in Sūrat al-Anfāl, Ayah 13, concerning the Battle of Badr. Being Shadīd al-'Iqāb (severe in punishment) does not contradict Allah being Arḥam ar-Rāḥimīn (the Most Merciful of the merciful), because each is in its proper place. In the Du'ā' al-Iftitāḥ, we read: «و ایقنت انّك أرحم الرّاحمین فى موضع العفو و الرحمة و أشدّ المعاقبین فى موضع النكال و النقمة» (And I am certain that You are the Most Merciful of the merciful in the place of pardon and mercy, and the most severe of punishers in the place of retribution and vengeance).

Messages

1- The minimum punishment for breaking a covenant is exile and expulsion from the homeland. (Banu Naḍīr deserved severe punishment due to conspiracy and treachery, but that decree was converted into their exile.) «لولا ان كتب اللّه علیهم الجلاء لعذّبهم فى الدنیا»

2- Hostility and confrontation caused the Divine wrath and the exile of Banu Naḍīr, not their being Jewish. «ذلك بانّهم شاقّوا اللّه»

3- Divine punishments are based on human actions and are just. «ذلك بانّهم شاقّوا اللّه»

4- Taking a stand against the heavenly leader is, in reality, taking a stand against Allah. (At the beginning of the Ayah, confrontation is against Allah and the Messenger, but at its conclusion, confrontation against Allah alone is mentioned.) «شاقّوا اللّه و رسوله... یشاقّ اللّه»

Ayah

مَا قَطَعْتُم مِّن لِّينَةٍ أَوْ تَرَكْتُمُوهَا قَآئِمَةً عَلَىٰٓ أُصُولِهَا فَبِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ وَلِيُخْزِىَ ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (٥)
Whatever palm trees you cut down or left standing on their roots, it was by Allah’s will and in order that He may disgrace the transgressors. (5)

Key Points

| "Līnah" (لینه) means softness, and it is also applied to the date palm tree, which bears a soft fruit. (11) Islam prohibits combatants from poisoning water and cutting down trees in enemy territory; however, sometimes the command to cut down trees is issued due to the tree obstructing the movement of the combatants or in order to strike a blow against the morale of the treacherous disbelievers. Perhaps the Ayah is a response to the Muslims' doubt regarding the correctness of their action, or to the objection raised by the Jews concerning the cutting of the trees, where the Quran states: This command was from Allah.

11) Mufradāt al-Rāghib.

Messages

1- In the battlefield, all actions must be under the supervision of the commander. «ما قطعتم... فباذن اللّه»

2- Under certain circumstances, the destruction of houses and the cutting of trees is necessary. «یخربون بیوتهم... قطعتم...»

3- Cutting down or not cutting down trees has limits that must be carried out with Divine authorization. «ما قطعتم... أو تركتم... فباذن اللّه»

4- A person's transgression (fisq) and corrupt beliefs affect the wastefulness of his property. «ما قطعتم... الفاسقین»

5- Harassing actions (intended to cause distress) are permissible on the battlefield. «لیخزى الفاسقین»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَفَآءَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِۦ مِنْهُمْ فَمَآ أَوْجَفْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ خَيْلٍ وَلَا رِكَابٍ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُسَلِّطُ رُسُلَهُۥ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (٦)
The spoils that Allah gave to His Apostle from them, you did not spur any horse for its sake, nor any riding camel, but Allah makes His apostles prevail over whomever He wishes, and Allah has power over all things. (6)

Key Points

«أفاء» (Afa'a) is derived from «فیئ» (fay') meaning return, and the intention is the wealth and spoils that come into the possession of the leader of the Muslims without fighting or bloodshed. Perhaps the reason it is called «fay'» is that the wealth returns to its original owners, who are the Divine Guardians (Awliya' Allah), because Allah created all the bounties of the world for the believers, and before them, for the Prophets. Although others may own it legally or customarily, the true owners are those great personalities.

«أوجفتم» (Awjaftum) means galloping with horses and camels. «خَیل» (Khayl) means horse, and «ركاب» (rikāb) means camel.

The wealth that comes into the possession of Muslims is of several types: A) Wealth acquired on the battlefield through war, which is called Ghanimah (spoils of war). The Mujahidin (fighters) own it, but they must pay its Khums (one-fifth). B) Wealth acquired without conflict or attack, which is called «Fay'», and its ruling is stated in this Ayah. C) Wealth such as barren lands (Mawāt), mountains, rivers, and reed beds, which is called Anfāl, and its ruling is mentioned at the beginning of Surah Al-Anfal.

Based on this Ayah, the wealth that accrues to the Muslims without galloping or raiding is at the disposal of the Prophet, not the Mujahidin. The Prophet distributes it among certain groups according to His discretion, as mentioned in the following Ayah.

The commands of Islam are wise. Where something is acquired through participation in the battlefield, they become the owners, but in cases where wealth is obtained without effort, it belongs to the heavenly leader.

Messages

1- In financial matters, even the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله does not dispose of wealth without the permission of Allah. «ما أفاء اللّه على رسوله»

2- Not all victories are achieved through war and Jihad. «فما أوجفتم من خيل ولا ركاب»

3- The dominance of the Guardians of Allah (Awliya' Allah) is valuable because it is by His will and command. «يسلّط رسله على من يشاء»

4- Where the Divine Will exists, fortresses and ramparts have no effect. «مانعتهم حصونهم... ولكن اللّه يسلط رسله على من يشاء»

Ayah

مَّآ أَفَآءَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِۦ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْقُرَىٰ فَلِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ وَلِذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ كَىْ لَا يَكُونَ دُولَةًۢ بَيْنَ ٱلْأَغْنِيَآءِ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَمَآ ءَاتَىٰكُمُ ٱلرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا نَهَىٰكُمْ عَنْهُ فَٱنتَهُوا۟ ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (۷)
The spoils that Allah gave to His Apostle from the people of the townships, are for Allah and the Apostle, the relatives and the orphans, the needy and the traveller, so that they do not circulate among the rich among you. Take whatever the Apostle gives you, and refrain from whatever he forbids you, and be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah is severe in retribution. (7)

Key Points

Dawlah (دولة) means the circulation and passing of wealth from hand to hand.

In the Qur'an, the necessity of economic support for the Prophet's Household (Ahl al-Bayt) علیهم السلام is mentioned four times, emphasizing the payment of a portion of Khums (one-fifth), Fay' (spoils without fighting), and other revenues to them: «و لذى القربى» (12), «و لذى القربى» (13), «و آت ذا القربى حقّه» (14), and «فآت ذا القربى حقّه» (15).

In the fourth Ayah of this Surah, the phrase «شدید العقاب» (Severe in punishment) was used for those who oppose the Prophet, and in this Ayah, the phrase «شدید العقاب» is also used for individuals who might object to the Prophet's distribution of Fay'.

In this Ayah, the ruling is stated first, «فللّه و للرسول و لذى...» (So it is for Allah and for the Messenger and for those...), then the reason for the ruling, «كى لایكون دولة...» (So that it may not become a possession...), and finally, attention is drawn to the Prophet's governmental decrees.

Allah has ordained both spiritual status for Dhul Qurba (the near kin) «قل لاأسئلكم علیه أجراً الا المودّة فى القربى» (16) and economic support for them. «ما افاء اللّه... لذى القربى»

Although six categories are mentioned in this Ayah for the distribution of Fay', it is not necessary for it to be divided equally among all categories (17).

Fay' is not exclusive to the person of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, but rather belongs to His position, status, and rank. Therefore, after the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله, it is in the hands of His rightful successors, meaning the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, and during the Occultation, it is in the hands of just jurists (fuqaha) who are free from desires and whims.

Wherever we acquire spoils (ghana'im) through the efforts of fighters, only one-fifth (Khums) of it is divided among the six groups, as stated in Ayah 41 of Surah Al-Anfal. But wherever something is acquired without effort, the entirety of it is divided among those six groups.

12) Al-Anfal, 41. 13) Al-Hashr, 7. 14) Al-Isra, 26. 15) Ar-Rum, 38. 16) Ash-Shura, 23. 17) Tafsir Ahsan al-Hadith.

Messages

1- The laws of the Qur'an are universal, not specific to the occasion or time of revelation. (The law of Fay' relates to all instances where the disbelievers have abandoned and left property, and is not specific to the Jews of Banu Nadir. The words «ما» (whatever) and «قرى» (towns) indicate generality.) «ما افاء اللّه على رسوله من اهل القرى»

2- The wealth of fleeing disbelievers is under the authority of the religious leader. «ما افاء اللّه على رسوله»

3- The Islamic system requires economic support, one source of which is Fay'—wealth that comes to Muslims without fighting, due to the flight or surrender of the disbelievers. «ما افاء اللّه على رسوله...»

4- Observing etiquette and proper status is necessary in speech. (First the name of Allah, then the Messenger of Allah, and then others). «للّه و للرسول...»

5- Allah's share is spent under the supervision of the Prophet in the way of pleasing Him. «فللّه...»

6- Poverty eradication is among the main programs of Islam. «و المساكین»

7- If the circulation of wealth among the rich Muslims is forbidden, then its circulation among the disbelievers is certainly prohibited. «كى لایكون دولة بین الاغنیاء...»

8- Economic planners in the Islamic system must pay attention to the moderation of wealth and its non-concentration in the hands of a specific group. «كى لایكون دولة بین الاغنیاء...»

9- Obedience to the Prophet's commands is obligatory, because the Prophet is infallible (ma'sum); otherwise, His instructions would not be absolutely and unquestionably obligatory to obey. «ما آتاكم الرسول فخذوه...»

10- The Sunnah (tradition) of the Prophet is mandatory to implement. «ما آتاكم الرسول فخذوه»

11- It is not necessary for all instructions to be mentioned in the Qur'an; rather, every command and prohibition found in the Prophet's sayings must be obeyed. «ما آتاكم الرسول فخذوه و ما نهاكم عنه فانتهوا»

12- We must act quickly in carrying out the Prophet's command. «فخذوه... فانتهوا» (The letter fa' (ف) indicates speed).

13- Financial matters and their distribution are a slippery slope that must be navigated with piety (taqwa). «ما افاء اللّه... و اتقوا اللّه»

14- Unquestioning obedience to the Prophet is a manifestation of piety (taqwa). «ما أتاكم الرسول فخذوه... و اتقوا اللّه»

15- Lack of piety leads to severe punishment. «و اتقوا اللّه انّ اللّه شدید العقاب»

16- Remembering the severe punishment is a cause for piety (taqwa). «و اتقوا اللّه... شدید العقاب»

Ayah

لِلْفُقَرَآءِ ٱلْمُهَـٰجِرِينَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُخْرِجُوا۟ مِن دِيَـٰرِهِمْ وَأَمْوَٰلِهِمْ يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلًا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرِضْوَٰنًا وَيَنصُرُونَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥٓ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلصَّـٰدِقُونَ (۸)
[They are also] for the poor Emigrants who have been expelled from their homes and [wrested of] their possessions, who seek grace from Allah and [His] pleasure and help Allah and His Apostle. It is they who are the truthful. (8)

Key Points

This Ayah serves as an explanation and commentary on the six categories of expenditure for Fay' (war booty acquired without fighting). It directs that from among the "destitute" (Masakin) and the "wayfarer" (Ibn al-Sabil), attention should be given to that group of the poor who possess merits such as migration (Hijrah) in the way of Allah and supporting the Messenger of Allah. Among the poor, the exiled migrants who sincerely uphold the religion have priority.

Messages

1- Although the needy individuals do not look toward our wealth, «یبتغون فضلاً من اللّه» (they seek bounty from Allah), we must still benefit them. «للفقراء»

2- Love for one's homeland is a natural and divine right that is worthy of being pursued/defended. «اخرجوا من دیارهم» (They were expelled from their homes).

3- Those who are truthful in their claim of religiosity are those who, despite poverty and displacement, support Allah and the Messenger. «للفقراء... اخرجوا... ینصرون اللّه... هم الصّادقون»

4- Praiseworthy are the virtues that are continuous. «یبتغون... ینصرون» (The imperfect tense [present/future] indicates continuity.)

5- This world holds value when accompanied by the Hereafter. «فضلا... و رضواناً» (Bounty... and pleasure).

6- Sincere individuals regard divine favors as His bounty, not as their own right. «فضلاً من اللّه»

7- Sincerity is made clear through action, not slogans. «ینصرون... الصّادقون»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ تَبَوَّءُو ٱلدَّارَ وَٱلْإِيمَـٰنَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ يُحِبُّونَ مَنْ هَاجَرَ إِلَيْهِمْ وَلَا يَجِدُونَ فِى صُدُورِهِمْ حَاجَةً مِّمَّآ أُوتُوا۟ وَيُؤْثِرُونَ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَلَوْ كَانَ بِهِمْ خَصَاصَةٌ ۚ وَمَن يُوقَ شُحَّ نَفْسِهِۦ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (۹)
[They are as well] for those who were settled in the land and [abided] in faith before them, who love those who migrate toward them, and do not find in their breasts any privation for that which is given to them, but prefer [the Immigrants] to themselves, though poverty be their own lot. And those who are saved from their own greed—it is they who are the felicitous. (9)

Key Points

"Tabawwu'" (تبوّؤ) is derived from "bawa'" (بواء), meaning settling down and taking residence in a place. (18) "Khasasah" (خصاصة) refers to poverty that causes a rift in one's life, and "yuqa" (یوق) is derived from "wiqayah" (وقایه), meaning protection and control. "Shuhh" (شُحّ) means stinginess accompanied by greed. (19)

Alongside benefiting the Muhajirun (Emigrants) from the fay' (spoils of war acquired without fighting), Allah praises the Ansar (Helpers) and the people of Medina, who both prepared their homes for the Muhajirun and perfected their faith by carrying out all commands without hesitation.

We read in a hadith: Imam Jawad عليه السلام would only recite one supplication from the beginning of the night until morning: «الّلهم قنى شُحّ نفسى» (O Allah! Protect me from the stinginess of my soul). The narrator says: I told the Imam, "May my life be sacrificed for you! I did not hear any supplication from you except this one!" The Imam replied: «فاىّ شى ء اشد من الشّحّ» (What thing is more dangerous than stinginess?).

In another hadith, we read: «لایجتمع الشحّ و الایمان فى قلب رجل مسلم» (Stinginess and faith do not gather in the heart of a Muslim man). (20)

Altruism (ithar) is a perfection, but it should not lead to exceeding the bounds of moderation, just as the Quran states in Ayah 67 of Surah Al-Furqan, describing the servants of the Most Merciful (Ibad ar-Rahman): «و الّذین اذا انفقوا لم یسرفوا و لم یقتروا و كان بین ذلك قواماً» (And [they are] those who, when they spend, do not spend excessively and do not restrict, but that is ever between that [extremity and restriction] a just balance). They neither spend excessively nor fall short in their spending; rather, they adopt a middle path. This matter has also been emphasized in a narration from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام. (21)

18) Tafsir Maraghi. 19) Mufradat Raghib. 20) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 21) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Preparing the ground for offering services to others is a value. «تبوّؤ الدار»
  2. Both an adorned exterior and an interior are necessary. «تبوّؤ الدار و الایمان»
  3. Love for the people of faith knows no borders. «یحبّون من هاجَرَ الیهم»
  4. Mention the altruism and good spirits of others. «یحبّون... یؤثرون»
  5. Lasting friendship is that in which we love individuals for their perfections, not for their name, tribe, or expectation of return. «یحبّون من هاجر»
  6. Pretending to have perfection is worthless; perfection is what resides deep within the soul. «لایجدون فى صدورهم حاجة»
  7. When praising one group, one should not neglect another group, lest sensitivity arise. The previous Ayah praised the Muhajirun, and this Ayah praises the Ansar. «یحبون من هاجر الیهم»
  8. More important than hospitality towards guests is friendship towards emigrants, because a guest stays only for a few days, but an emigrant remains with a person for a long time. «یحبون من هاجر الیهم»
  9. In honoring people, hierarchy and precedence must be maintained. First the Muhajirun (previous Ayah), then the Ansar (this Ayah), and in the next Ayah, the Tabi'in (Successors) are praised.
  10. Allah praises individuals who do not harbor envy. (Envy usually manifests during the distribution of wealth. Even though the Prophet gave the fay' only to the Muhajirun, the Ansar had no expectations.) «لایجدون... حاجة مما اوتوا»
  11. Noble character and broad vision are praised by the Quran. «لایجدون فى صدورهم حاجة»
  12. Expressing friendship is not enough; practical friendship is necessary. «یحبّون... یؤثرون»
  13. Giving despite one's own need is more important. «یؤثرون... و لو كان بهم خصاصة»
  14. Being saved from stinginess is the source of salvation. «مَن یوق شحّ نفسه... هم المفلحون»
  15. Stinginess is intertwined with the soul. If someone is kept away from stinginess, he can sacrifice not only his wealth but also his life. «یوق شحّ نفسه»
  16. The stingy person will not attain salvation. «مَن یوق شحّ نفسه فاولئك هم المفلحون»
  17. Salvation from stinginess is possible through divine help and assistance. The word «یوقَ» (is protected) is in the passive voice because the Protector is Allah.
  18. Many perfections are related to being saved from stinginess. The phrase «یوق شح نفسه» (is protected from the stinginess of his soul) appears at the end of the Ayah as a key.

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ جَآءُو مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا ٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَلِإِخْوَٰنِنَا ٱلَّذِينَ سَبَقُونَا بِٱلْإِيمَـٰنِ وَلَا تَجْعَلْ فِى قُلُوبِنَا غِلًّا لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱٠)
And [also for] those who came in after them, who say, ‘Our Lord, forgive us and our brethren who were our forerunners in the faith, and do not put any rancour in our hearts toward the faithful. Our Lord, You are indeed most kind and merciful.’ (10)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the Muhajirun (Emigrants) and the Ansar (Helpers) were praised, and this verse speaks of the Tabi'in (Followers), which can include all Muslims after the Prophet.

Verses 8 to 10 point to factors of unity: 1. Love. «یحبّون من هاجر الیهم» 2. Altruism/Selflessness. «و یؤثرون على انفسهم» 3. Supplication (Du'a). «ربّنا اغفر لنا... ربّنا» 4. Service. «تبَوّءُو الدار» 5. Avoiding greed and envy. «لایجدون فى صدورهم حاجة مما اوتوا...» 6. Avoiding stinginess. «یوق شحّ نفسه» 7. Brotherhood (Ukhuwwah). «اغفر لنا و لاخواننا» 8. Broad-mindedness and lack of malice. «لاتجعل فى قلوبنا غلاّ للذین آمنوا»

Forgiveness (Maghfirah) and seeking forgiveness (Istighfar): The topic of forgiveness and its related words have been mentioned in the Quran more than two hundred times. We must both seek forgiveness ourselves and ask the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) to seek forgiveness for us. «یا ابانا استغفر لنا» (O our father, ask forgiveness for us) (22). Even the angels seek forgiveness for the believers. «و یستغفرون للذین آمنوا» (And they ask forgiveness for those who have believed) (23). However, seeking forgiveness for polytheists (Mushrikun) and hypocrites (Munafiqun) has no effect. «سواء علیهم استغفرت لهم ام لم تستغفر لهم لن یغفر اللّه لهم» (It is the same for them whether you ask forgiveness for them or do not ask forgiveness for them; never will Allah forgive them) (24). «ما كان للنبىّ و الّذین آمنوا ان یستغفروا للمشركین» (It is not for the Prophet and those who have believed to ask forgiveness for the polytheists) (25).

But the factors leading to Divine forgiveness are: 1. Obedience to the heavenly leader. «فاتّبعونى یحببكم اللّه و یغفر لكم ذنوبكم» (So follow me, [then] Allah will love you and forgive you your sins) (26). 2. Piety (Taqwa). «ان تتقوا اللّه... یغفر لكم» (If you fear Allah... He will forgive you) (27). 3. Pardon and overlooking people's faults. «و لیعفوا و لیصفحوا الا تحبون ان یغفر اللّه لكم» (And let them pardon and overlook. Would you not like that Allah should forgive you?) (28). 4. Sound and firm speech. «قولوا قولا سدیداً... یغفر لكم» (Speak a word to the point... He will forgive you) (29). 5. Goodly loan (Qardh Hasan). «ان تقرضوا اللّه... یغفر لكم» (If you loan Allah... He will forgive you) (30). 6. Repentance (Tawbah) and seeking forgiveness (Istighfar). «و من یعمل سوءً أو یظلم نفسه ثمّ یستغفر اللّه یجد اللّه غفوراً رحیما» (But whoever does wrong or wrongs himself then seeks forgiveness of Allah will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful) (31).

22) Yusuf, 97. 23) Ghafir, 7. 24) Munafiqun, 6. 25) Tawbah, 113. 26) Aal-i-Imran, 31. 27) Anfal, 29. 28) Nur, 22. 29) Ahzab, 70 - 71. 30) Taghabun, 17. 31) Nisa, 110.

Messages

  1. A complete school of thought is one that has a plan for perfection and growth for its future followers as well. «و الّذین جاءوا من بعدهم»
  2. To prevent misunderstanding, we should first seek forgiveness for our own faults, and then for the faults of others. «اغفر لنا و لاخواننا»
  3. A believer does not have immunity from sin, but whenever he commits a sin, he immediately seeks forgiveness. «اغفر لنا و لاخواننا»
  4. Praying for the deceased is the duty of those who come after them. «و الّذین جاؤوا من بعدهم یقولون ربّنا اغفر... الّذین سبقونا بالایمان»
  5. All Muslims are brothers to one another and pray for each other. «اغفر لنا و لاخواننا الّذین سبقونا بالایمان»
  6. Precedence in faith is a value. «سبقونا بالایمان»
  7. True brotherhood is illuminated by faith. «اخواننا الّذین سبقونا بالایمان»
  8. In religious fraternity and brotherhood, time, place, and race are irrelevant. «اخواننا الّذین سبقونا بالایمان»
  9. We should remove malice from our hearts through good supplication. «ربّنا اغفر... لاخواننا... لاتجعل غلاً للذین آمنوا»
  10. A believer does not wish ill upon others. «لاتجعل فى قلوبنا غلاً للذین آمنوا»
  11. Self-purification cannot be achieved without seeking help from Allah. «ربّنا... لاتجعل فى قلوبنا غلاً»
  12. In reforms, one must address the roots. The root of many sins is malice, envy, and enmity, which must be eradicated. «لاتجعل فى قلوبنا غلاً»
  13. Attention to the Lordship (Rububiyyah), kindness (Ra'fat), and Mercy (Rahmah) of Allah is among the etiquette and guarantee for the acceptance of supplications. «ربّنا اغفر... انّك رؤف رحیم»

Ayah

۞ أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ نَافَقُوا۟ يَقُولُونَ لِإِخْوَٰنِهِمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَئِنْ أُخْرِجْتُمْ لَنَخْرُجَنَّ مَعَكُمْ وَلَا نُطِيعُ فِيكُمْ أَحَدًا أَبَدًا وَإِن قُوتِلْتُمْ لَنَنصُرَنَّكُمْ وَٱللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّهُمْ لَكَـٰذِبُونَ (۱۱)
Have you not regarded the hypocrites who say to their brethren, the faithless from among the People of the Book, ‘If you are expelled, we will surely go out with you, and we will never obey anyone against you, and if you are fought against we will surely help you,’ and Allah bears witness that they are indeed liars. (11)

Key Points

After praising the Muhajirun (Emigrants), the Ansar (Helpers), and the Tabi'in (Successors), these verses depict the face of the hypocrites (Munafiqun) and the disbelievers (Kuffar) who stand side-by-side against them. Indeed, one side invites to forgiveness and pardon, while the other invites to obstinacy and resistance in disbelief and godlessness. We read in the commentaries (Tafasir): The hypocrites sent a message to the treacherous Jews, who had incurred the wrath of the Prophet of Islam, saying, "Do not abandon your stronghold, for we are with you." Considering the verse, «انما المؤمنون اخوة» (32), which restricts brotherhood solely to the people of faith, it must be said that the brotherhood between the hypocrite and the disbeliever is a formal and superficial brotherhood. «لاخوانهم الّذین كفروا...» In true brotherhood, sacrifice and selflessness are evident, just as the Ansar demonstrated towards the Muhajirun, «و یؤثرون على انفسهم». But in superficial brotherhood, even outward help is absent. «لننصرنّكم و اللّه یشهد انهم لكاذبون»

32) Al-Hujurat, 10.

Messages

1- The leader of the Islamic society must be aware of the relations between internal hypocrites and external enemies. «الم تَرَ»

2- In the early days of Islam, there were hypocrites who maintained secret and treacherous relations with the disbelievers and gave them hope through their promises. «الم تَرَ الى الّذین نافقوا...»

3- The hypocrite is the brother of the disbeliever. «نافقوا... لاخوانهم الّذین كفروا»

4- Not all People of the Book stand against the Muslims; only a group is engaged in conspiracy and enmity. «الّذین كفروا من اهل الكتاب»

5- The lying hypocrite presents his speech decisively to make others believe him. «لنخرجنّ معكم... لننصرنّكم»

6- The hypocrite is not a follower or obedient to the Islamic leadership in political and social matters. «لا نطیع فیكم احداً ابداً»

7- The hypocrites lie even to their own friends. «و اللّه یشهد انهم لكاذبون»

Ayah

لَئِنْ أُخْرِجُوا۟ لَا يَخْرُجُونَ مَعَهُمْ وَلَئِن قُوتِلُوا۟ لَا يَنصُرُونَهُمْ وَلَئِن نَّصَرُوهُمْ لَيُوَلُّنَّ ٱلْأَدْبَـٰرَ ثُمَّ لَا يُنصَرُونَ (۱۲)
Surely, if they were expelled they will not go out with them, and if they were fought against they will not help them, and [even if] they were to help them they will turn their backs [to flee] and eventually they will not be helped. (12)

Messages

1- Awareness of the disbelievers' defeat and the hypocrites' lack of commitment to their pledges strengthens the morale of the Muslims. «لایخرجون معهم... لاینصرونهم»

2- Do not fear the bombastic propaganda of the hypocrites, for they are not men of action and will very quickly abandon the field. «لایخرجون... لاینصرون... لیولّن الادبار»

3- The covenant-breaking of the hypocrites is such that they are not even loyal to their allied disbelievers. «لایخرجون... لاینصرون»

4- The consequence of hypocrisy is humiliation and loneliness. «لاینصرون»

Ayah

لَأَنتُمْ أَشَدُّ رَهْبَةً فِى صُدُورِهِم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَفْقَهُونَ (۱۳)
Indeed they have a greater awe of you in their hearts than of Allah. That is because they are a lot who do not understand. (13)

Key Points

"Rahbah" (رهبة) means deep, internal fear and terror, the effects of which become manifest in action. This Ayah serves as the proof for the previous Ayah, which stated that the hypocrites are not willing to fight, and if they come to the battlefield, they will flee, because they lack faith and do not recognize Divine power, but they see the flash of the sword and fear you more than they fear Allah.

Messages

1- The believer possesses awe and majesty. «لانتم اشد رهبة فى صدورهم»

2- Fearing people instead of fearing Allah is a clear sign of hypocrisy. «لانتم اشد رهبة فى صدورهم من اللّه»

3- The hypocrites do not know or understand that the secret to the Muslims' honor and power is the will and grace of Allah. «لایفقهون»

4- Fearing people and not fearing Allah is due to superficiality and foolishness. «لانتم اشدّ رهبة... لایفقهون»

Ayah

لَا يُقَـٰتِلُونَكُمْ جَمِيعًا إِلَّا فِى قُرًى مُّحَصَّنَةٍ أَوْ مِن وَرَآءِ جُدُرٍۭ ۚ بَأْسُهُم بَيْنَهُمْ شَدِيدٌ ۚ تَحْسَبُهُمْ جَمِيعًا وَقُلُوبُهُمْ شَتَّىٰ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَعْقِلُونَ (۱٤)
They will not fight against you together except in fortified townships or from behind walls. Their fierceness is great only within themselves. You suppose them to be united, but their hearts are divided. That is because they are a lot who do not exercise their reason, (14)

Key Points

Qurā (قُرى) is the plural of Qaryah (قریة) and refers to a place where people gather, whether it is a city or a village. Muḥaṣṣanah (محصنة) comes from Ḥiṣn (حِصن), meaning a fortress or castle. Qurā Muḥaṣṣanah (قرىً محصنة) means areas that are protected by walls, trenches (moats), or towers. The value, role, and blessings of unity and solidarity become clear to everyone with a little thought and reasoning. Therefore, the end of the Ayah states: «قوم لایعقلون» (a people who do not reason). However, realizing that all power belongs to Allah and that we should not fear humans more than Allah requires pure and deep knowledge (Ma'rifah) and Monotheism (Tawhid). Therefore, the previous Ayah concluded with «قوم لایفقهون» (a people who do not understand).

Messages

1- Assessing the morale of the enemy and their allies, as well as the enemy's method of warfare, is necessary. «لایقاتلونكم جمیعاً الا... تحسبهم جمیعاً و...»

2- Be careful that the hypocrites do not establish areas for themselves that are protected and immune from your reach, as their danger becomes serious in that case. «لایقاتلونكم... الا فى قرى محصّنة»

3- Superficial alliances that lack inner faith and inclination are worthless. «تحسبهم جمیعاً و قلوبهم شتّى»

4- Material and physical resources and equipment are not the sole factors for security. Conflict and insecurity exist among the disbelievers despite all their equipment. «قرىً محصنة... بأسهم بینهم شدید»

5- Humiliating and weakening the hypocrites and the enemy is necessary. «قوم لایفقهون... قوم لایعقلون»

6- Disagreements during war are a sign of lack of reason. «لایقاتلونكم جمیعاً... قلوبهم شتى... لایعقلون»

Ayah

كَمَثَلِ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ قَرِيبًا ۖ ذَاقُوا۟ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱٥)
just like those who tasted the evil consequence of their conduct recently before them, and there is a painful punishment for them. (15)

Key Points

«وبال» (Wabāl) means the evil consequences of an affair. A believer is not stung twice from the same hole, but the Jews of Banu Nadir were deceived by the promises of the hypocrites and did not consider that these hypocrites had given the exact same promises to the Jews of Banu Qaynuqa' a short while before and had failed to fulfill them.

Messages

1- When identifying individuals and groups, refer to their history. «كمثل الّذین من قبلهم»

2- The leader of the society must be aware of the history of nations and groups. «كمثل الّذین من قبلهم»

3- In propagation (Da'wah) and education, use examples that are easier for people to understand. «قریباً»

4- Material hardships are a reflection of the punishment of the disbelievers. «ذاقوا وبال أمرهم»

5- History possesses fixed laws and traditions (Sunan). If the disbelievers followed a path, they tasted its bitter flavor; if you follow it, you will taste it too. «ذاقوا وبال أمرهم»

6- Hardships and blessings are the result of our own actions. «وبال امرهم»

7- Cooperation with hypocrites brings punishment both in this world and in the Hereafter. «ذاقوا وبال أمرهم و لهم عذاب الیم»

Ayah

كَمَثَلِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ إِذْ قَالَ لِلْإِنسَـٰنِ ٱكْفُرْ فَلَمَّا كَفَرَ قَالَ إِنِّى بَرِىٓءٌ مِّنكَ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ ٱللَّهَ رَبَّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٦)
[The hypocrites are] like Satan when he tells man to disbelieve, but when he disbelieves, he says, ‘I am absolved of you. Indeed I fear Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.’ (16)

Ayah

فَكَانَ عَـٰقِبَتَهُمَآ أَنَّهُمَا فِى ٱلنَّارِ خَـٰلِدَيْنِ فِيهَا ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَٰٓؤُا۟ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱۷)
So the fate of both is that they will be in the Fire, to remain in it [forever]. Such is the requital of the wrongdoers. (17)

Key Points

This verse, following the previous one, likens the hypocrites to Satan, who constantly promises people, but His promises are nothing but deception. How numerous are the individuals and Satanic governments that give promises to their followers, but abandon them when danger arises.

Ibn Abbas narrates the following story regarding these verses: In Bani Israel, there was an ascetic (worshipper) named Barsisa who worshipped for years and became famous, and people brought their sick to him for healing and treatment. Until one day, they brought a noblewoman to him. Satan tempted him, and he sexually assaulted the woman. Then he killed her and buried her in the desert. The woman's brothers found out, the matter became widespread, and the ascetic fell from his position. The ruler of the time summoned him, he confessed to his sin, and the verdict was issued that he should be hanged. At this moment, Satan materialized before him, saying, "My temptation brought you to this state. If you prostrate to me, I will free you." The ascetic said: "I am unable to prostrate." Satan said: "Prostrate to me with a gesture of your eyebrow." He did so, completely losing his faith, and was ultimately killed (33).

Satan misleads everyone in a different way and distances them from the path: The ascetic of Bani Israel falls into sin through his worship. Qarun becomes arrogant due to his knowledge and management. «على علم عندى» (34) Bal'am Ba'ura, despite possessing the Greatest Name (Ism al-A'zam), pursues his desires. «آتیناه آیاتنا فانسلخ منها» (35) The Samiri becomes arrogant due to his knowledge and skill. «بصرت بما لم یبصروا» (36) One becomes arrogant due to his wealth and riches. «فلما آتاهم من فضله بخلوا به» (37) Another due to his sons. «و بنین شهودا» (38) Another due to governance. «ألیس لى ملك مصر» (39) And another due to equipment and fortifications. «جابوا الصخر بالواد» (40), «تنحتون من الجبال بیوتا» (41)

Similarities between Hypocrites and Satan 1. Both are enemies of man: «انّه لكم عدو مبین» (42), «هم العدو» (43) 2. Both call people to immorality and evil: «یأمركم بالفحشاء» (44), «یأمرون بالمنكر» (45) 3. Both must be avoided: «و لا تتّبعوا خطوات الشیطان» (46), «فاحذروهم» (47) 4. Both are two-faced and deceptive in appearance: «كمثل الشّیطان اذ قال للانسان اكفر... قال انّى برى منك», «و اذا لقوا الّذین آمنوا قالوا آمنّا و اذا خلوا الى شیاطینهم قالوا انّا معكم» (48) 5. Both deceive under the guise of goodwill: «هل ادلّك عى شجرة الخلد» (49), «قالوا انّما نحن مصلحون» (50) 6. The abode of both is Hell: «انّهما فى النّار» (51), «انّ المنافقین فى الدرك الاسفل من النّار» (52)

Messages

1- To prove a point or to have a greater impact on the audience, one can use multiple examples and experiences. «كمثل الّذین من قبلهم... كمثل الشّیطان»

2- The temptations and instigations of the hypocrites are an example of the temptations of Satan. «كمثل الّذین... كمثل الشّیطان»

3- Satan only invites to deviation; it is man who accepts the deviation by his own choice. «اكفر... فلما كَفَر»

4- Satan is a companion only halfway through the journey. «اكفر فلما كفر قال انى برى ءٌ»

5 - The state of a faithless person reaches a point where even Satan disavows him. «انّى برى ءٌ منك»

6- Satan and the hypocrites use the fear of Allah as a justification for breaking promises and withholding help. «انّى اخاف اللّه» The ultimate end of hypocrisy and disbelief is eternal fire.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلْتَنظُرْ نَفْسٌ مَّا قَدَّمَتْ لِغَدٍ ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (۱۸)
O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah, and let every soul consider what it sends ahead for Tomorrow, and be wary of Allah. Allah is indeed well aware of what you do (18)

Key Points

The command for Taqwa (piety/God-consciousness) is repeated and emphasized in this Ayah. In addition to emphasis, this repetition can carry multiple meanings, including: 1. The first Taqwa relates to the principle of the action, and the second Taqwa relates to its quality. 2. The first Taqwa relates to performing good deeds, and the second Taqwa relates to abandoning forbidden acts (muharramat). 3. And perhaps the first Taqwa relates to repentance for the past, and the second Taqwa relates to preparing provisions for the future. We read in narrations: Gladden hearts even with half a date, or if you do not have that, then with kind words, so that you are not ashamed on the Day of Judgment when Allah asks: I gave you eyes, ears, and... what provision did you send forth? For on that Day, some will look in every direction, but they will not have sent forth any provision that could be a means of their salvation.(53)

53) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Faith is the foundation of Taqwa, and having Taqwa is the condition for faith to be fruitful. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا اتّقوا»

2- We should not rely on the good deeds of our heirs; everyone must think of their own Day of Judgment. «ولتنظر نفس ما قدّمت لغد»

3- We must be precise regarding what we store up as righteous deeds. «ولتنظر نفس ماقدّمت»

4- Self-reckoning (muhasabat al-nafs) is necessary. «و لتنظر نفس» (If we are not careful today, the realization tomorrow will lead to shame. «یوم ینظر المرء ما قدّمت یداه و یقول الكافر یا لیتنى كنت ترابا»(54))

5- The Day of Judgment is near. «لغد» (Just as He says elsewhere: «انهم یرونه بعیدا و نراه قریبا»(55))

6- Foresight and consideration of the ultimate outcome are prerequisites of faith. «ما قدّمت لغد»

7- Taqwa provides the basis for self-reckoning, and self-reckoning leads to the growth and strengthening of Taqwa. («و لتنظر نفس» is placed between the two instances of «اتّقوا اللّه»).

8- No one should consider himself safe at any stage. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا اتقوا اللّه... و اتقوا اللّه»

9- Belief in Allah being All-Aware (Khabir) is a factor leading to Taqwa. «واتقوا اللّه... ان اللّه خبیر»

54) An-Naba', 40.

55) Al-Ma'arij, 6.

Ayah

وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ نَسُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ فَأَنسَىٰهُمْ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ (۱۹)
Do not be like those who forget Allah, so He makes them forget their own souls. It is they who are the transgressors. (19)

Ayah

لَا يَسْتَوِىٓ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ وَأَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۚ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ هُمُ ٱلْفَآئِزُونَ (۲٠)
Not equal are the inmates of the Fire and the inhabitants of paradise. It is the inhabitants of paradise who are the successful ones. (20)

Key Points

The clear example of those who forgot Allah are the hypocrites. In Ayah 67 of Surah At-Tawbah, concerning the hypocrites, we read: «نسوا اللّه فنسیهم» (They forgot Allah, so He forgot them). Of course, Allah is not forgetful; rather, He withholds His grace from them. «و ما كان ربّك نسیّا» (And your Lord is never forgetful) (56). Negligence (Ghaflah) is the most dangerous affliction for man. The Quran states: A group of people are worse than cattle because they are heedless: «أولئك كالانعام بل هم أضل أولئك هم الغافلون» (Those are like cattle; nay, they are even more astray. Those are the heedless ones) (57). Therefore, heedlessness of Allah «نسوا اللّه» (58), heedlessness of the Day of Reckoning «نسوا یوم الحساب» (59), and heedlessness of the Divine signs «اتتك آیاتنا فنسیتها» (60) cause man to fall to a stage lower than animality. Whoever forgets Allah inevitably becomes an individual without a path, without a leader, without a goal, and without law, drowning in desires. All his goals and actions become arbitrary, conforming to his own inclinations and whims, and this is the greatest danger for man. Whoever forgets His grace becomes despairing and reaches a dead end. Whoever forgets His pardon does not see himself as capable of reform and engages in every kind of deed. Whoever forgets the Divine leaders follows every tyrant and becomes corrupt. Whoever forgets His law seeks a new voice every day and becomes confused. Whoever forgets His path becomes bewildered among hundreds of other paths and goes astray. Whoever forgets His Awliya (saints/friends) is manipulated by bad companions. Whoever forgets Allah's blessings becomes a flatterer of others. Whoever forgets Allah will inevitably forget His wise goals regarding the creation of man, and whoever forgets the purpose of his creation wastes his life, capital, and talents. Man is inherently a knower of Allah (God-conscious), and forgetfulness is something that afflicts him. The word nisyan (forgetfulness) applies to a situation where there was prior knowledge and attention.

(56) Maryam, 64. (57) A'raf, 179. (58) Tawbah, 67. (59) Sad, 26. (60) Taha, 126.

Messages

1- Believers are exposed to heedlessness regarding the remembrance of Allah and need warnings. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا اتقوا اللّه... و لاتكونوا كالّذین نسوا اللّه»

2- The first step towards downfall originates from man himself; self-forgetfulness is a Divine punishment. «نسوا اللّه فانساهم»

3- Divine punishment is commensurate with the deed. «نسوا اللّه فانساهم أنفسهم»

4- Sin and deviation are the fruits of heedlessness regarding the remembrance of Allah. «نسوا اللّه فانساهم أنفسهم أولئك هم الفاسقون»

Ayah

لَوْ أَنزَلْنَا هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ عَلَىٰ جَبَلٍ لَّرَأَيْتَهُۥ خَـٰشِعًا مُّتَصَدِّعًا مِّنْ خَشْيَةِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَتِلْكَ ٱلْأَمْثَـٰلُ نَضْرِبُهَا لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (۲۱)
Had We sent down this Quran upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled [and] go to pieces with the fear of Allah. We draw such comparisons for mankind, so that they may reflect. (21)

Messages

1- One of the methods of education is indirect reprimand. «لو أنزلنا هذا القرآن... تلك الامثال نضربها...»

2- The Quran, being a Divine Book, has no deficiency; rather, people lack the necessary receptivity. «لو أنزلنا هذا...»

3- Using analogy (parable) to express spiritual truths is a Quranic method. «لو أنزلنا هذا القرآن على جبل...»

4- How is it that the mountain, with all its solidity, is submissive before the greatness of the Quran, yet the hearts of some humans are harder than stone? «لو أنزلنا هذا القرآن على جبل لرأیته خاشعاً...»

5- Existence possesses consciousness. If the Quran had been revealed upon the mountain, humility and awe would have manifested from it. «لرأیته خاشعاً متصدّعاً من خشیة اللّه»

6- Quranic parables and analogies require contemplation and reflection; otherwise, they will not be properly understood. «تلك الامثال نضربها للنّاس لعلّهم یتفكّرون»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ عَـٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ ۖ هُوَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۲۲)
He is Allah—there is no god except Him—Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, He is the All-beneficent, the All-merciful. (22)

Key Points

In narrations (Riwāyāt), great emphasis has been placed on reciting the final verses of Sūrah Al-Ḥashr, and various blessings have been mentioned for it, including the forgiveness of sins and reaching the rank of a martyr (Shahīd). It is probable that a person may attain the Greatest Name (Ism al-A'ẓam) among the Names mentioned in these verses (22-24), because these verses contain approximately fifteen, or by some consideration, eighteen Attributes of Allah, each of which indicates His infinite perfections. The true object of worship (Ma'būd) is an existence that possesses knowledge, mercy, sanctity, might ('Izzah), power, grandeur ('Aẓamah), and complete encompassing (Iḥāṭah). Since these attributes belong exclusively to Allah, there is no object of worship except Him. Wherever the Unseen (Ghayb) and the Seen (Shahādah) appear together in the Qur'an, the Unseen is mentioned first, followed by the Seen. This points either to its importance or to the frequency of its occurrence. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «الغیب ما لم یكن و الشهادة ما قد كان»(61) The Unseen (Ghayb) is that which has not yet occurred, and the Seen (Shahādah) is that which has already come into existence.

Messages

1- Uniqueness and oneness are the first and most important principles among the Divine Attributes. «هو اللّه الّذى لا اله الاّ هو»

2- He Whom everyone seeks is Allah. «هو اللّه»

3- Allah's knowledge of the Unseen (Ghayb) and the Seen (Shahādah) is identical. «عالم الغیب و الشّهادة»

4- Although Allah knows everything, «عالم الغیب و الشّهادة», He deals with His servants with mercy. If His knowledge inspires fear, His mercy inspires hope, and in spiritual training, a person must maintain a balance between fear and hope. «الرّحمن الرّحیم»

5- Every being other than the One God lacks extensive knowledge and mercy. «هو... عالم الغیب... هو الرحمن الرحیم»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْقُدُّوسُ ٱلسَّلَـٰمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُ ٱلْمُهَيْمِنُ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْجَبَّارُ ٱلْمُتَكَبِّرُ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (۲۳)
He is Allah—there is no god except Him—the Sovereign, the All-holy, the All-benign, the Securer, the All-conserver, the All-mighty, the All-compeller, and the All-magnanimous. Clear is Allah of any partners that they may ascribe [to Him]! (23)

Key Points

"Al-Malik" (The King) means the Owner of people's affairs and the possessor of authority over their governance. "Al-Quddūs" (The Holy) means pure and exalted above every flaw and deficiency. "Al-Muhaymin" (The Guardian/Overseer) means the possessor of dominance, control, and supervision. "Al-Jabbār" (The Compeller/Restorer) has come with two meanings: capable of compulsion (Jabr) and the Restorer (Compensator). "As-Salām" (The Source of Peace) means the One who deals with peace and well-being, not with war, conflict, or evil and harm. "Al-Mu'min" (The Giver of Security) also means the One who grants you security and preserves you in His protection. (62) Human rulers are entirely weakness, need, limitation, and deficiency, and people are not safe from them. But Allah the Almighty is a Sovereign who possesses holiness and is far removed from every flaw and deficiency; He is also the Giver of security, He has dominance and influence over everything, He possesses impenetrable power, He is the Restorer, and He possesses grandeur and majesty.

61) Tafsir Rahnama; Ma'ani al-Akhbar, p. 146. 62) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The sole object of worship in existence is the One who possesses absolute sovereignty. «هو اللّه الذى لا اله الاّ هو الملك»

2- The sovereignty of Allah is far removed from all forms of oppression and deficiency and is full of holiness. «القدوّس السلام»

3- Allah inflicts no harm whatsoever upon His creation. «السلام»

4- Allah has complete encompassment over existence. «المهیمن»

5- The conjectures of the polytheists regarding Allah are incorrect. «سبحان اللّه عما یشركون»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْخَـٰلِقُ ٱلْبَارِئُ ٱلْمُصَوِّرُ ۖ لَهُ ٱلْأَسْمَآءُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۲٤)
He is Allah, the Creator, the Maker, and the Former. To Him belong the Best Names. Whatever there is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Him and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (24)

Key Points

Al-Bāriʾ (the Originator) means the Creator whose creations are distinct and differentiated from one another. This is the only Surah whose beginning and end are marked by the glorification (Tasbīh) of Allah. "سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ..." (Sabbaha lillāhi...) and "يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ..." (Yusabbihu lahu...). Its first Ayah speaks of might (Izzah) and wisdom (Hikmah), and its last Ayah concludes with Al-Azīz Al-Hakīm (the Mighty, the Wise).

Messages

1- Allah is a Creator Who, in creating His creatures, has not copied anyone or anything; rather, He Himself is the Shaper (Mussawwir) of phenomena. "الخالق البارى ء المصوّر"

2- He Who possesses all perfections is worthy of the glorification (Tasbīh) of the entire universe. "لَهُ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَىٰ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ..."

3- The glorification of creatures is a sign of a type of knowledge and consciousness existing in the universe. "يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ"

4- The glorification of creatures is a reminder to those humans who do not engage in glorification (Tasbīh). "يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ"

5- The power of Allah is accompanied by wisdom, not oppression or transgression. "هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ"
